hypothesis: If a ∆-learning approach is used to predict the correction between computationally inexpensive GFN1-xTB semi-empirical band gaps and high-level DFT (PBE or HSE06) band gaps using XGBoost with structural and chemical descriptors, then it will achieve higher accuracy in predicting DFT-level band gaps for metal-organic frameworks than either direct machine learning models trained on DFT band gaps alone or deep learning benchmarks, because the semi-empirical method captures the dominant physical trends while the machine learning correction systematically accounts for the residual errors, reducing the complexity of the learning task.
